December 2016, York City, five nominees Cinema Honors Heterodox Award were announced today, honoring films that actively blur line between narrative fiction documentary.
first time Cinema history, films that nominated Cinema Eye's nonfiction film craft honors also nominated Heterodox Award.
nonfiction nominations announced last month Brooklyn, These Sleepless Nights nominated Outstanding Outstanding Original Score Kate Plays Christine nominated Outstanding Direction.
Both films debuted Documentary Competition 2016 Sundance Film Festival, critics audiences have debated films' place line ever since.
other three nominees continue celebration international cinema that Heterodox award known for.
Fits Neon Bull debuted 2015 Venice International Film Festival, Mountains Depart celebrated World Premiere Cannes that same year.
This marks seventh year Heterodox Award Cinema Eye.
Previous winners award Matt Porterfield's Putty Hill (2011), Mike Mills' Beginners (2012), Cohen's Museum Hours (2013), Carlos Reygados's Post Tenebras (2014), Richard Linklater's Boyhood (2015) Jafar Panahi's Taxi (2016).
With announcement this year's Heterodox nominees, this year's Cinema nominated films filmmakers have been revealed.
recipient this year's Legacy Award will announced coming weeks.
finalists Heterodox Award were selected voting Cinema Honors Nominations Committee, made more than international programmers specialize nonfiction film.
finalists were then viewed five nominees were selected second round committee, composed nonfiction programmers journalists.
second round included Scott Macaulay (Editor Chief, Filmmaker Magazine), Hadrian Belove (Executive Director, Cinefamily), Eric Hynes (Associate Curator Film, Museum Moving Image), Doug Jones (Executive Director, Images Cinema), Mads Mikkelsen (Programmer, CPH:DOX), Nuxoll (Program Director, Rooftop Films), Rachael Rakes (Film Critic, Brooklyn Rails) Alison Willmore (Film Critic, Buzzfeed).
Honors Ceremony centerpiece Cinema Week, multi-day, multi-city celebration that acknowledges best work nonfiction film through screenings events.
final four days Cinema Week take place York City, where series celebratory events brought together many year's most accomplished filmmakers.
This year's dates January 8-11, with awards presented Museum Moving Image Queens January 2017.
Nonfiction film nominations 10th Annual Cinema Honors were announced last month Alamo Drafthouse Brooklyn.
